title: Ground Gingered Vietnamese Beef Com Tam
description: A jammy and crunchy Vietnamese dish featuring minced beef, served with com tam, a popular Vietnamese street food, this recipe is flexitarian-friendly and full of flavor.
instructions: In a large mortar and pestle, pound the sliced ginger until well bruised and fragrant.
Add the ground beef to the mortar and pestle, and pound until just combined with the ginger.
In a small bowl, whisk together the fish sauce, lime juice, and brown sugar, until the sugar is dissolved.
Add the sauce to the mortar and pestle, and stir until the beef is evenly coated.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat, and cook the beef mixture, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks, until it is no longer pink, about 5-7 minutes.
To assemble the com tam, place a scoop of cooked com tam on a plate, followed by a spoonful of the beef mixture.
Garnish with thinly sliced scallions, fried shallots, and a drizzle of chili sauce, if using.
